response.justification: Maria is a strong senior individual contributor with genuinely relevant developer-tooling experience — she has built test runners, assertion libraries, CI infrastructure, and device-orchestration tools, which maps well to the engineering-productivity focus. However, this is a people-management role requiring at least 2 years of formal people management and 3 years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing engineers, running one-on-ones, owning performance reviews, or leading organizational change. Her mobile experience is limited to one iOS/Apple TV stint years ago. She would be a compelling candidate for a senior/staff IC role on this team, but not for the manager position as scoped.
strengths: Deep developer-tools background: test runners (Trial), assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD setup, and device-orchestration tooling
15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: No formal people-management experience — no direct reports, performance management, or team development on record
No evidence of organizational-change leadership or strategic roadmap ownership at team/org level
Mobile-platform experience is dated and shallow (one iOS/Apple TV role in 2015-2016) for a mobile-ecosystem-focused org

key_factors.factor: Strong developer-tooling and testing-infrastructure track record
